name: Asclepius
shortDescription: Asclepius is the ancient Greek god of medicine, healing, rejuvenation, and physicians. He represents the healing aspect of the medical arts.
description: Asclepius is the Greek god of medicine and healing, revered for his ability to cure the sick and even raise the dead. He is the son of Apollo and Coronis, a mortal woman. According to myth, Coronis was unfaithful to Apollo and was killed, but Apollo rescued the unborn Asclepius from her womb. He was then raised by the centaur Chiron, who taught him the art of medicine and surgery. Asclepius became so skilled that he could restore life to the dead, a power that angered Zeus, who feared that Asclepius's abilities would disrupt the natural order of life and death. Zeus struck him down with a thunderbolt. After Asclepius's death, Apollo avenged his son by killing the Cyclopes who had forged Zeus's thunderbolts. Asclepius's cult flourished throughout the ancient world, with temples dedicated to him serving as healing centers. These temples, known as Asclepieions, were places where the sick would seek cures through rituals, incubation (sleeping in the temple in hopes of receiving a divine dream), and medical treatments. The most famous Asclepieion was located at Epidaurus. Asclepius is typically depicted with a staff entwined with a snake, known as the Rod of Asclepius, which remains a symbol of medicine today. His daughters include Hygieia (goddess of health, cleanliness, and hygiene), Panacea (goddess of remedies), Iaso (goddess of recuperation), Aceso (goddess of the healing process), and Aglaea (goddess of beauty, splendor, glory, magnificence, and adornment).
